frameworks.name: dynamic causal modeling
frameworks.one_line: infer effective connectivity between parietal regions and V5/MT during bistable perception

frameworks.name: hierarchical parietal network model
frameworks.one_line: r-aSPL and r-pSPL play opposite hierarchical roles in triggering perceptual reversals

core_question: How do right anterior and posterior superior parietal regions interact with each other and visual area V5/MT during spontaneous perceptual reversals?
key_assumptions: r-aSPL and r-pSPL have opposing causal roles in perceptual switches
bistable structure-from-motion provides spontaneous perceptual reversals without stimulus change
effective connectivity can be inferred from fMRI BOLD via DCM
manipulations: viewing a bistable structure-from-motion stimulus while fMRI is recorded
modeling reciprocal couplings among r-aSPL, r-pSPL, and V5/MT
key_findings: resolving perceptual ambiguity is associated with reciprocal interactions between the two parietal regions and V5/MT
bottom-up coupling strength from V5/MT to r-pSPL and from r-pSPL to r-aSPL predicts individual mean dominance duration
interpretations: parietal-V5/MT effective connectivity dynamics underlie spontaneous perceptual switches in bistable vision
